steps.title: Identify what slope means on a scatterplot
steps.content: The slope of a line of best fit equals the rise divided by the run between any two points on the line. Read two clear points from the line, compute the ratio, then match to the closest answer choice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $0$: A slope of 0 is a horizontal line. The line of best fit clearly rises from left to right, so the slope is positive and nonzero. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{1}{2}$: Using approximate points on the line such as $(0, 2)$ and $(4, 9)$, the slope is $\frac{9-2}{4-0} = \frac{7}{4} \approx 1.75$. A slope of $\frac{1}{2}$ is far too shallow. ✗
  • Choice C — $1$: A slope of 1 would mean equal rise and run, but the line rises about 7 units over 4 horizontal units — much steeper than 1. ✗
  • Choice D — $2$: The computed slope $\approx 1.75$ is closest to 2 among the four options. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
